questions.question: The value of \( {{16 × 9 ÷ 4 of 2} \over 3 × (9- 6)2×3÷4of\frac{1}{2}}\)is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. ⅔. The value of \( {{16 × 9 ÷ 4 of 2} \over 3 × (9- 6)2×3÷4of\frac{1}{2}}\) Using BODMAS here ⇒ (16 × 9 ÷ 4 of 2)/(3 × (9 - 6)2 × 3 ÷ 4 of ½ ⇒ (16 × 9 ÷ 8)/(3 × (9 - 6)2 × 3 ÷ 2) ⇒ (2 × 9)/(3 × (9 - 6) × 3) ⇒ 18/(3 × 3 × 3) ⇒ ⅔

questions.question: Two circles touch each other externally. The radius of the first circle with centre A is18 cm. The radius of the second circle with centre B is 8 cm.Find the length of their common tangent CD.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 24 cm. Common tangent = √[D² - (R - r)²] Common tangent = √[26² - (18 - 8)²] = √(676 - 100) = √576 = 24 cm

questions.question: A thief takes off on his bike at a certain speed, after seeing a police car at a distance of 250 m. The police car starts chasing the thief and catches him. If the thief runs 1.5 km before being caught and the speed of the police car is 70 km/h, then what is the speed of the thief’s bike (in km/h)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 60. The initial distance between the thief and the police = 250 m = 0.25 km The thief runs 1.5 km before being caught. So, the police car covers = 1.5 + 0.25 = 1.75 km The speed of the police car = 70 km/h Time taken by the police = 1.75/70 = 0.025 hours Thief's speed = 1.5/0.025 = 60 km/h So, the speed of the thief's bike is 60 km/h.

questions.question: The marked price of an article is Rs. 9900. If a discount of 25 percent is given, then what is the selling price of the article?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Rs. 7425. MP = Rs.9900 Discount = 25% SP = MP × (100 - D)/100 ⇒ 9900 × 75/100 = 7425

questions.question: If\(sin\theta={{11} \over 15}\) , then the value of \(({{sec\theta - tan\theta}})\) is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. \({{\sqrt26} \over 13}\) sinθ = 11/15 sinθ = P/H = 11/15 H² = P² + B² 225 = 121 + B² B = 2\(\sqrt{26}\) \(({{sec\theta - tan\theta}})\) = [15/( 2\(\sqrt{26}\))] - [ 11/( 2\(\sqrt{26}\))] = 2/\(\sqrt{26}\) × (\(\sqrt{26}\)/\(\sqrt{26}\)) = \({{\sqrt26} \over 13}\)

questions.question: The radius of a sphere is 9 cm. It is melted and drawn into a wire of radius 0.3 cm. The length of the wire is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 108 m. Let the length of the wire be h. According to the given question, Volume of sphere = Volume of wire ⇒ (4/3)π × 9 × 9 × 9 = π × 0.3 × 0.3 × h ⇒ h = 10800 cm = 108 m

questions.question: The population of a city is 3,52,314. It increases annually at the rate of 15% per annum. What was its population 2 years ago?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 2,66,400​​​​​. According to the question: Let the population two years ago be x. ⇒ x × (100 + 15)/100 × (100 + 15)/100 = 352314 ⇒ x × 115/100 × 115/100 = 352314 ⇒ x = (352314 × 100 × 100)/(115 × 115) ⇒ x = 266400

questions.question: A man spends 72% of his salary and saves Rs 5,740 per month. What is 10% of his annual salary (in Rs)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 24600​​​​. Calculations: A man spends 72% of his salary and saves Rs 5,740 per month Savings (%) = 100 - 72 = 28% According to the question; ⇒ 28% = 5740 ⇒ 1% = 205 His salary = 100% = 20500 Annual salary = 12 × 20500 = 246000 Hence, 10% of his annual salary = 10% of 246000 = Rs. 24600

questions.question: Pipe A is a filling pipe, while B and C are emptying pipes. Pipe A alone can fill a tank in 10 hours and pipe B alone can empty two third part of the tank in 20 hours. If all three pipes are opened together, the tank is completely filled in 40 hours. In how many hours can pipe C alone empty two-third part of the tank?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 16. B alone empty two third of the tank in 20 hours B alone empty the whole tank = 20 × 3/2 = 30 hours Total work (LCM of 10, 30 and 40) = 120 units Efficiency of A (120/10) = 12 units Efficiency of B (120/30) = 4 units (negative) Efficiency of (A - B - C) (120/40) = 3 Efficiency of C (12 - 4 - 3) = 5 units Two-third part of the tank = 120 × ⅔ ⇒ 80 units Required time = 80/5 = 16 hours

questions.question: When the same number is subtracted from each of 7, 9, 11, and 15, the resulting numbers are in proportion. The number subtracted is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 3. Let the given numbers be a, b, c, and d Then, a/b = c/d let x be subtracted from each number ⇒ (7 - x)/(9 - x) = (11 - x)/(15 - x) ⇒ (7 - x)(15 - x) = (11 - x)(9 - x) ⇒ 105 - 7x - 15x + x² = 99 - 11x - 9x + x² ⇒ 105 - 22x = 99 - 20x ⇒ 6 = 2x ⇒ x = 3

questions.question: The following figure shows the percentage growth in units of fans and coolers sold in some years. What is the difference between the averages of the growth rate in coolers and fans? ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/DI_13-08-2024_D182024_08_13_03_39_43.PNG)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 34. Given that The total growth rate in sales of coolers = 60 + 72 + 54 + 45 = 231 The average growth rate in sales of coolers = 231/4 The total growth rate in sales of fans = 27 + 18 + 30 + 20 = 95 The average growth rate in sales of fans = 95/4 Hence, The required difference = 231/4 - 95/4 = 136/4 = 34

questions.question: A, B, and C can independently complete the same task in 4, 6, and 12 days, respectively. In how many days will they be able to complete the same task if they work together?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 2. Total work = LCM (4, 6, 12) = 12 days Efficiency ratio of A, B, C = (12/4) : (12/6) : (12/12) = 3 : 2 : 1 They together can complete the task = 12/(3 + 2 + 1) = 12/6 = 2 days

questions.question: If the nine - digit number 23541y49x is divisible by 72 then, (3x + 5y) : (5x + 3y) is equal to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 7 : 9. 23541y49x is divisible by 72, 72 being the product of 8 and 9, which are two co-prime. So the number has to be divisible by both 8 and 9. 23541y49x is divisible by 8, if 49x is divisible by 8, which gives x = 6 The number becomes 23541y496, which will be divisible by 9 if the sum of the digits becomes divisible by 9 Now, (2 + 3 + 5 + 4 + 1 + y + 4 + 9 + 6) = 34 + y ⇒ y should be 2 so that the digit sum becomes (34 + 2 = 36) which is divisible by 9 For x = 6, y = 2 (3x + 5y) ∶ (5x + 3y) ⇒ (3 × 6 + 5 × 2) : (5 × 6 + 3 × 2) ⇒ (18 + 10) : (30 + 6) ⇒ 28 : 36 = 7 : 9

questions.question: If in △ABC, AB = AC and ∠ACD = 125°, then ∠BAC is: ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Screenshot%202025-02-27%202354302025_02_27_23_55_25.png)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 70°. ∠ACB = 180º - 125º = 55º AB = AC then ∠ABC = 55º ∠BAC = 180º - 2 × 55º ⇒ 180º - 110º = 70º

questions.question: A can do a piece of work in 12 days and B can do it in 30 days. They work on alternate days; starting with A. In how many days will the work be completed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. \(16 \dfrac 4 5\). Total work (LCM of 12 and 30) = 60 units Efficiency of A = 60/12 = 5 units Efficiency of B = 60/30 = 2 units As they work on alternate days so, Work done in 2 days = 7 units Work done in 16 days = 56 units Remaining units = 60 - 56 = 4 units A will complete the remaining work = (⅘) days Total days = 16 + (⅘) = \(16\dfrac{4}{5}\) days

questions.question: A chair is bought for Rs. 1500 and is sold at 25 percent loss. What is the selling price of the chair?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Rs. 1125. C.P. of the chair = Rs.1500 Loss on selling the chair = 25% Selling price = Cost price × (100 - loss %)/100 S.P. = 1500 × (100 - 25)/100 ⇒ S.P. = 1500 × 75/100 ⇒ S.P. = 15 × 75 ⇒ S.P. = Rs. 1125

questions.question: The area of the sector of a circle is 128 cm². If the length of the arc of that sector is 64 cm, then find the radius of the circle.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 4 cm Area of a sector = lr/2 where, l = arc length, r = radius of a circle Area a sector = 64 × r/2 = 128 ⇒ 32 × r = 128 ⇒ r = 128/32 = 4 cm

questions.question: Find the value of y in the expression: \(2\frac{3}{7} \times [(11\frac{2}{6}\div({25\over18} + 2\frac{5}{6} - {7\over36}))] = y \div 72.5\times204\)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. \(\frac{17}{7}\). ⇒ \(2\frac{3}{7} \times [(11\frac{2}{6}\div({25\over18} + 2\frac{5}{6} - {7\over36}))] = y \div 72.5\times204\) ⇒ \({17\over7} \times [{68\over6}\div({25\over18} + {17\over6} - {7\over36})] = {y\over 72.5}\times204\) ⇒ \({17\over7} \times [{68\over6}\div({50\over36} + {102\over36} - {7\over36})] = {y\over 72.5}\times204\) ⇒ \({17\over7} \times [{68\over6}\div{145\over36}] = {y\over 72.5}\times204\) ⇒ \({17\over7} \times [{68\over6}\times{36\over145}] = {y\over 72.5}\times204\) ⇒ \({17\over7} \times [{68}\times{6\over145}] = {y\over 72.5}\times204\) ⇒ \({17\over7} \times {408\over145} = {y\over 72.5}\times204\) ⇒ \({17\over7} \times{408\over145} \times{72.5\over204} = y\) ⇒ y = \(\frac{17}{7}\) or 2.42

questions.question: If the sum of two quantities is equal to six times their difference, then find the ratio of the two quantities.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 7 : 5. Given: The sum of two quantities is equal to six times their difference Explanation: According to the given question, x + y = 6(x - y) ⇒ x + y = 6x - 6y ⇒ x - 6x = -6y - y ⇒ -5x = -7y ⇒ 5x = 7y ⇒ x/y = 7/5 Required ratio = 7 : 5

questions.question: The monthly income of two persons is in the ratio 4 : 5 and their expenditures are in the ratio 7 : 9. If each saves Rs. 50 per month, then their monthly incomes are:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Rs. 400; Rs. 500. Let the monthly income of one person be 4a and that of the other be 5a Let the monthly expenses of one person be 7b and that of another be 9b According to the question; ⇒ 4a - 7b = 50 .........(1) ⇒ 5a - 9b = 50........(2) On solving both equations, b = 50, a = 100 Monthly income of one person = 4 × 100 = Rs. 400 Monthly income of other person = 5 × 100 = Rs. 500 Therefore, the monthly incomes of both persons are Rs. 400 and Rs. 500.

questions.question: The diameter of a semicircle is 5.6 m. What is its perimeter (in m, correct to one decimal place)? (Take \(\pi =\frac{22}{7}\).)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 14.4. Radius of the given semicircle = 5.6/2 = 2.8 m Circumference of the semicircle = 22/7 × 2.8 + 2 × 2.8 = 14.4 m Hence, the perimeter of the semicircle = 14.4

questions.question: The HCF of two numbers is 6 and their LCM is 253080. If one of the numbers is 4218, what is the second number?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 360. one number × second number = HCF × LCM ⇒ 4218 × second number = 6 × 253080 ⇒ second number = 253080/703 = 360

questions.question: Ankit sells a chair at a loss of 25 percent. If he sells it for Rs. 900 more, then he earns a profit of 5 percent. What should be the selling price of the chair to earn a profit of 40 percent?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Rs. 4200. Let, the C.P. of the chair be Rs.x. Ankit sells a chair at a loss of 25 percent = 0.75x If he sells it for Rs. 900 more, then he earns a profit of 5 percent ⇒ 0.75x + 900 = 1.05x ⇒ 1.05x - 0.75x = 900 ⇒ 0.3x = 900 ⇒ x = Rs.3000 Selling price at 40% profit ⇒ S.P. = x + 0.40x = 1.40x ⇒ 1.40 × 3000 = Rs.4200 Thus, the S.P. of the chair to earn a profit of 40% is Rs. 4200.

questions.question: If sin (a + b) = 1 and cos (a - b) = \(\frac{1}{2}\), then find a.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e 75°. ⇒ sin (a + b) = 1 ⇒ sin(a + b) = sin 90° ⇒ a + b = 90°....(1) ⇒ cos(a - b) = cos ½ ⇒ cos(a - b) = cos 60° ⇒ a - b = 60°....(2) Solving eq 1 and 2 ⇒ 2a = 150° ⇒ a = 75°

questions.question: The average weight of 12 person's increases by 1.5 kg when a new person comes in place of one of them weighing 45 kg. What will be the weight of the new person?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 63 kg. Let the average weight of 12 persons is x kg and the weight of the new person is y kg. If one person replaces the new person then the sum of 12 persons = 12x - 45 + y The new sum of the weights of 12 persons = 12(x + 1.5) Now, 12x - 45 + y = 12(x + 1.5) ⇒ y = (45 + 18) = 63 kg So, weight of new person = 63 kg

questions.question: One diagonal of a rhombus is 8\(\sqrt{3}\) cm. If the other diagonal is equal to its side, then the area (in cm²) of the rhombus is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 32\(\sqrt{3}\). Area of a rhombus = ½ × Diagonal1 × Diagonal2 ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_10.05.2022_D43.png) By Pythagoras theorem a² = a²/4 + (4√3)² ⇒ a² = 64 ⇒ a = 8 cm = D₂ The area of the rhombus = ½ × 8 × 8√3 ⇒ 32√3 sq. cm

questions.question: If x³ = 270 + y³ and x = (6 + y), then what is the value of (x + y)? (given that x > 0 and y > 0)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. \(4\sqrt3\). x³ = 270 + y³ ⇒ x³ - y³&ⁿᵇˢᵖ;= 270...................1 x = (6 + y) ⇒ x - y = 6......................2 (x - y)³ = x³ - y³ - 3xy(x - y) ⇒ 6³ = 270 - 3xy(6) ⇒ 216 - 270 = -18xy ⇒ xy = 54/18 = 3 (x + y)² = (x - y)² + 4xy ⇒ (x + y)² = (6)² + 4 × 3 ⇒ (x + y)² = 48 ⇒ (x + y) = \(4\sqrt3\)

questions.question: When an integer n is divided by 6, the remainder is 5. What is the remainder if 9n is divided by 6?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 3. n = 6 × quotient + 5 9n = 9(6 × quotient + 5) ⇒ 9 × 6 × quotient + 5 × 9 ⇒ 9 × 6 × quotient + 7 × 6 + 3 ⇒ 6(9quotient + 7) + 3 3 is the remainder if 9n is divided by 6

questions.question: Sunil and Gopal are business partners. Sunil invests Rs. 20,000 for 9 months and Gopal invests Rs. 30,000 for 12 months. Find Sunil's share of the profit of Rs. 60,000.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Rs 20000. Total amount = Investment amount × Period Sunil's investment = 20000 × 9 = 180000 Gopal's investment = 30000 × 12 = 360000 Profit = S : G = 180000 : 360000 = 1 : 2 Total profit = 1 + 2 = 3 ⇒3 = 60000 (given) Profit amount of Sunil = (60000/3) × 1 = Rs 20000

questions.question: Ten observations written in descending order are 45, 34, 32, 30, 2x - 13, x + 1, 17, 15, 14 and 8. If the median is 24, then find the value of 'x'.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 20. The observation s in descending order: 45, 34, 32, 30, 2x - 13, x + 1, 17, 15, 14 and 8 We know that, Median = [(n/2)ᵗʰ term + (n/2 + 1)ᵗʰ term]/2, [n = 10] Median = [(10/2)ᵗʰ term + (10/2 + 1)ᵗʰ term]/2 Median = [5ᵗʰ term + 6ᵗʰ term]/2 ⇒ 24 = ((x + 1) + (2x - 13))/2 ⇒ 48 = 3x - 12 ⇒ 3x = 60 ⇒ x = 20

questions.question: If the first half of a journey is done at a speed of 60 km/h and the second half at a speed of 40 km/h then, find the average speed.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 48 km/h. Average Speed = 2xy/(x + y) here, x = 60 and y = 40 Now, Average Speed = 2 × 60 × 40/(60 + 40) ⇒ 4800/100 = 48 km/hr

questions.question: If x(5 - \(\frac{2}{x}\)) = \(\frac{5}{x}\), then the value of x² + \(\frac{1}{x^2}\) is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. \(\frac{54}{25}\). x (5 - \(\frac{2}{x}\)) = \(\frac{5}{x}\) ⇒ 5x - 2 = \(\frac{5}{x}\) ⇒ 5(x - \(\frac{1}{x}\)) = 2 ⇒ x - \(\frac{1}{x}\) = ⅖ Square on both sides ⇒ x² + \(\frac{1}{x^2}\) = (4/25) + 2 = \(\frac{54}{25}\)

questions.question: A invested an amount of ₹12,000 in a fixed deposit scheme for 2 years at an interest rate of 5% per annum, compounded annually. How much amount will A get on maturity of the fixed deposit?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. ₹13,230 Amount after 2 years = Principal × (1 + rate/100)² ⇒ Amount after 2 years = 12000 × (1 + 5/100)² ⇒ Amount after 2 years = 12000 × (21/20)² ⇒ Amount after 2 years = 12000 × {441/400} ⇒ Amount after 2 years = 30 × 441 = 13,230

questions.question: 10 years ago the average age of a man and his wife was 42 years. Now the average age of the family consisting of him, his wife and their son is 39 years. The present age of the son is ____.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 13 years. Son's present age = (Sum of present ages of family) - (Sum of present ages of husband and wife) = (39 × 3) - [(42 × 2) + 10 + 10] = 117 - 104 = 13 Years

questions.question: What is the simple interest on Rs.4,840 at 6% per annum for 10 years?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Rs.2,904. Explanation: The formula for calculating the simple interest is S.I. = (P × R × T)/100 According to the question, P = Rs.4,840, R = 6%, T = 10 years ⇒ S.I. = (4840 × 6 × 10)/100 ⇒ S.I. = 484 × 6 ⇒ S.I. = 2904 Hence, the simple interest is Rs.2,904

questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-clusters are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd man out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. EUX. Logic: 1st letter ⇔ 2nd letter; 2nd letter + 2 = 3rd letter Except EUX, all words follow the same logic. While EUX: E ⇔ V ≠ U; V + 2 = X

questions.question: Directions: A question is given, followed by two statements labelled I and II. Identify which of the statements is/are sufficient to answer the question. Five people, A, B, C, D and E, have different weights each. Who is the heaviest? (I) A is heavier than C but lighter than D. (II) B is not the heaviest and E is not the lightest.
options: Both Statements I and II together are sufficient to answer the question.
Data in Statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question, while data in Statement II is not.
Both Statements I and II together are not sufficient to answer the question.
Data in Statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question, while data in Statement I is not.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Both Statements I and II put together are not sufficient to answer the question. (I) A is heavier than C but lighter than D: D > A > C Thus, statement (I) alone is not sufficient to answer the question. (II) B is not the heaviest and E is not the lightest. Thus, statement (II) alone is not sufficient to answer the question. After combining both statements: D > A > C Position of B and E is not cleared so both Statements I and II together are not sufficient to answer the question.

questions.question: If ' × ' means '+', '+' means ' - ', ' - ' means '÷' and '÷' means ' × ', what will be the value of the following expression? [{(92 - 4) ÷ 6} + (54 ÷ 2)] × 28
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 58. Given expression: [{(92 - 4) ÷ 6} + (54 ÷ 2)] × 28 After replacing the signs, we get [{(92 ÷ 4) × 6} - (54 × 2)] + 28 = [{23 × 6} - (54 × 2)] + 28 = [138 - 108] + 28 = 30 + 28 = 58

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'FAITH' is coded as '9-1-18-4-10', and 'MOUNT' is coded as '16-18-18-16-4'. How will 'CHARGE' be coded in that language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 9-10-1-9-13-8. Logic: If the alphabet is consonant - its positional value is squared. If the alphabet is a vowel - its positional value is cubed. FAITH → 6-1-9-20-8 → 36-1-729-400-64 MOUNT → 13-15-21-14-20 → 169-3375-9261-196-400 Then each letter is finally coded as the sum of the digits of the number representing it. FAITH → 9-1-18-4-10 MOUNT → 16-18-18-16-4 Similarly, CHARGE → 3-8-1-18-7-5 → 9-64-1-324-49-125 → 9-10-1-9-13-8 Hence, the correct answer is 9-10-1-9-13-8.

questions.question: D and E are daughters-in-law of A and B, whose son is C. H is the son of F, whose wife is E. How is E related to C?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Sister-in-law. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_25-04-2025_D402025_04_25_19_29_36.png) From the above tree, E is the sister-in-law of C.

questions.question: One day morning, Ganesh and Shiva were facing to each other, where Shiva's shadow fell on the left side of Ganesh. Which side was Shiva facing?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. South. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Quant_01-11-2024_D292024_11_01_18_24_49.png) So, Shiva is facing south.

questions.question: Directions: In each of the following questions, a statement is given followed by two assumptions. You have to decide which assumption(s) are implicit in the given statement. Statement: “Buy pure butter of Company A.” - an advertisement in a magazine. Assumptions: I. No other company supplies pure butter. II. People read advertisements.
options: if only assumption I is implicit
if only assumption II is implicit
if either I or II is implicit
if neither I nor II is implicit
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. If only assumption II is implicit. I. Not implicit - According to the statement, Company A makes pure batter, but it does not mention that any company does not make pure batter. II. Implicit - Yes, People always read advertisements before buying anything.

questions.question: Direction: In the given statement, relationship between different elements is shown and it is followed by two conclusions. Choose the correct answer on the basis of the information given below. Statement: Z > L ≥ P ≥ M ≤ C = N Conclusions: I. M ≥ Z II. M
options: If either conclusion I or II follows
If neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. If only Conclusion II follows. I. M ≥ Z: False (Z > L ≥ P ≥ M ≤ C = N II. M True (Z > L ≥ P ≥ M ≤ C = N

questions.question: Select the word-pair that best represents a similar relationship to the one expressed in the pair of words given below. (The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must NOT be related to each other based on the number of letters/number of consonants/vowels in the word) Kalinga Cup : Football
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Divan Cup : Badminton. Kalinga Cup is a specific trophy associated with the sport of Football. Similarly, Divan Cup is a specific trophy associated with the sport of Badminton. Note: Davis Cup is associated with Lawn tennis. Nehru Trophy is associated with the Boat Race. Ezra Cup is associated with Polo.

questions.question: Directions: A statement is given followed by two inferences I and II. You have to consider the statement to be true even if it seems to be at variance from commonly known facts. You have to decide which of the given inferences, if any, follow from the given statement. Statement: The city administration has imposed a fine on households that waste water through overflowing tanks or unattended taps. Inferences: I. Water scarcity is a concern for the city administration. II. The city administration wants citizens to use water more responsibly.
options: Both inferences I and II follow
Neither inference I nor II follows
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Both inferences I and II follow. Inferences: I: Follows → The act of penalizing water wastage implies that the city is concerned about conserving water. II. Follows → Imposing a fine for wastage clearly signals an intent to encourage responsible behavior.

questions.question: Seven people, A, B, C, D, E, F and G are sitting in a row, facing north. Only five people sit between F and D. G sits third to the right of F. E is the immediate neighbour of C and D. B is not the immediate neighbour of G. How many people sit between A and F?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. One. According to the information, the arrangement is: ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Savita_ALP_Seating_D22025_01_29_10_17_03.png) Thus, one people sit between A and F.

questions.question: Q, B, L, V, N, X and G has a lectures on a different day of the week starting from Monday and ending on Sunday. Only one person has a lecture between X and V. X has a lecture on Wednesday. Only one person has a lecture between L and B. B has a lecture on Thursday. G has a lecture on the day immediately after B. Q has a lecture just before Wednesday. N has a lecture on which day?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Sunday. Person Day V Monday Q Tuesday X Wednesday B Thursday G Friday L Saturday N Sunday Thus, N has a lecture on Sunday.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 22. The hour and minute hand coincide i.e. at "0 degrees" is only once between 11 and 1 o'clock i.e. at 12 o'clock. For 12 hours they will coincide 11 times. So, for 24 hours they will coincide 22 times.

questions.question: Two trains are running late by 4 minutes and 6 minutes respectively further, it is getting delayed by 4 seconds and 6 seconds every minute. Find that after how many minutes this train will be delayed by one full hour?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 840 minutes and 540 minutes. We know that, 1 hours = 60 minutes For 4 min delayed already, then 60 - 4 = 56 minutes = 56 × 60 = 3360 seconds ∵ train is delayed by 4 seconds in 1 minute ∴ train will be delayed by 3360 seconds = 3360 /4 = 840 minutes For 6 min delayed already, then 60 - 6 = 54 minutes = 54 × 60 = 3240 seconds ∵ train is delayed by 6 seconds in 1 minute ∴ train will be delayed by 3240 seconds = 3240 /6 = 540 minutes

questions.question: Which Mahajanapada was associated with Lord Buddha's life and teachings?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Kosala. The Kosala and Magadha Mahajanapadas are deeply associated with the life and teachings of Lord Buddha. He was born in Lumbini, which was part of the Kosala region, and spent his early years in Kapilavastu, the capital of the Shakya clan, a part of Kosala. King Prasenajit of Kosala was a notable supporter of Buddhism during Buddha's time. Magadha played a crucial role later in Buddha's life. He attained enlightenment under the Bodhi tree in Bodh Gaya, located in Magadha. The first Buddhist Sangha was formed in Rajgir, the capital of Magadha, under King Bimbisara, another ardent follower of Buddha. Many of Buddha’s sermons and teachings were delivered in this region, marking Kosala and Magadha as pivotal in his journey and the spread of Buddhism.

questions.question: The rulers of which Rajput dynasty supported Jainism, with one ruler said to have embraced Jainism under the influence of the Jain scholar Hemachandra?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Chalukya. The rulers of the Chalukya dynasty of Gujarat were notable patrons of Jainism. Several Chalukya kings extended significant support to Jain scholars and temple construction. Notably, Kumarapala, a prominent Chalukya ruler, is reported to have embraced Jainism under the profound influence of the esteemed Jain scholar Hemachandra. Hemachandra's teachings and his prominent position in the Chalukya court led to Jainism flourishing during Kumarapala's reign. • Pratihara: While some Pratihara rulers might have shown tolerance towards Jainism, their primary patronage was directed towards Hindu deities, particularly Vishnu and Shiva. • Parmara: The Parmara dynasty's rulers were primarily Shaivites, with significant patronage extended to Hindu temples and scholars. • Chahman (Chauhan): The Chahman rulers were primarily associated with Hindu deities, although some instances of tolerance towards other religions might have existed. However, they are not as prominently linked to the patronage of Jainism as the Chalukyas of Gujarat.

questions.question: Allan Octavian Hume obtained permission for the first session of the Indian National Congress from which of the following Viceroys of India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Lord Dufferin. Lord Dufferin, the Viceroy of India (1884–1888) granted permission for the first session of the Indian National Congress (INC) in 1885. Allan Octavian Hume, a retired British civil servant, played a pivotal role in establishing the INC as a platform for Indian political dialogue under Lord Dufferin's tenure. The first session of the Indian National Congress (1885, Bombay) was chaired by Womesh Chandra Bonnerjee, attended by 72 delegates, and focused on constitutional reforms under British rule. The second session (1886, Calcutta), led by Dadabhai Naoroji, expanded participation to 434 delegates and emphasized economic grievances like unfair taxation. The third session (1887, Madras), under Badruddin Tyabji's presidency, marked the first Muslim leader at the helm, advocating unity among Indians while addressing issues like civil rights and administrative reforms. Other Options: • Lord Ripon (1880–1884) – Served as Viceroy before Lord Dufferin; introduced local self-government (Resolution of 1882) but left before the INC's formation. • Lord Curzon (1899–1905) – Known for partitioning Bengal (1905) and antagonizing the INC, which grew more assertive during his rule. These early sessions laid the foundation for India's organized nationalist movement.

questions.question: Who among the following Mughal rulers introduced the Mansabdari system that became the basis of Mughal military organisation and civil administration?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Akbar. The Mansabdari system, a defining feature of Mughal governance, was instituted by Emperor Akbar as part of his visionary administrative reforms in the late 16th century. This innovative framework revolutionized the empire's military and civil administration by creating a unified, merit-based hierarchy that integrated diverse ethnic and religious groups into a cohesive imperial service. Under this system, every official—from provincial governors to military commanders—was assigned a numerical rank (mansab), with dual components: the zat (personal pay grade) and sawar (cavalry obligations). Akbar's genius lay in making these ranks transferable and reviewable, preventing the emergence of hereditary power centers while ensuring loyalty to the crown. The system's financial mechanics were equally sophisticated, with compensation through revenue assignments (jagirs) or cash salaries, coupled with strict inspections to maintain military readiness. By incorporating Rajput chiefs and other indigenous elites into this structure, Akbar not only strengthened the empire's military capabilities but also fostered political integration across religious lines. The Mansabdari system's flexibility allowed successors like Jahangir and Shah Jahan to adapt it to changing needs, though its later corruption under Aurangzeb—particularly the rampant sale of ranks and jagir crises—contributed to administrative decay. Other Options: • Aurangzeb: He continued and expanded the Mansabdari system, but his reign saw its stresses, leading to administrative and economic challenges. • Babur: As the founder of the Mughal Empire, he laid its foundations and introduced gunpowder warfare but did not establish the formal Mansabdari system. • Jehangir: He inherited and refined the Mansabdari system from his father, Akbar, introducing concepts like 'Du-aspa Sih-aspa' to enhance its efficiency.

questions.question: Who among the following represented Congress during the Second Round Table Conferences?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. None of the above. The Indian National Congress (INC) was officially represented by Mahatma Gandhi at the Second Round Table Conference (1931) in London. Here’s a concise breakdown: • First Round Table Conference (1930): Congress boycotted it due to the absence of Purna Swaraj demands. • Second Conference (1931): Gandhi participated as the sole Congress delegate after the Gandhi-Irwin Pact. • Third Conference (1932): Congress did not attend, but leaders like Madan Mohan Malaviya opposed the Communal Award separately. Other Options: • Motilal Nehru: He was a prominent leader of the Indian National Congress and played a crucial role in drafting the Nehru Report (1928), but he passed away in February 1931, before the Second Round Table Conference. • Jawaharlal Nehru: While a key figure in the Congress and the independence movement, he did not attend the Second Round Table Conference as the Congress's representative; Mahatma Gandhi was the sole official representative. • Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel: A stalwart leader and close associate of Gandhi, Sardar Patel was the President of the Indian National Congress at the Karachi Session in March 1931, which approved the Gandhi-Irwin Pact and decided that Gandhi would represent the Congress at the Second Round Table Conference. He himself did not attend the conference.

questions.question: KK Venugopal, the 15 Attorney General of India, received the first extension in his tenure in which of the following years?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 2020. KK Venugopal, the 15th Attorney General of India, received his first extension in tenure in 2020. He was initially appointed in July 2017 and his term was extended for another year in June 2020. Venugopal is known for his extensive experience in constitutional law and has represented the Indian government in various high-profile cases. His reappointment was notable for continuing his significant contributions to legal matters in the Supreme Court of India during his extended tenure.

questions.question: The Balwant Rai Mehta Committee recommended in 1957 for the establishment of a ___________ Panchayati Raj System in India.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Three-tier. The Balwant Rai Mehta Committee, established in 1957, recommended the establishment of a three-tier Panchayati Raj system in India. This system consists of the Gram Panchayat at the village level, the Panchayat Samiti at the block level, and the Zila Parishad at the district level. The committee aimed to promote democratic decentralization, enabling local self-governance and enhancing community participation in development activities. Its recommendations were crucial in shaping the framework for local governance in India, ultimately leading to the formal implementation of Panchayati Raj institutions across various states.

questions.question: What is the schedule of the Indian Constitution related to anti-defection law?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Tenth Schedule. The Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution relates to the Anti-Defection Law. • Introduction: The Tenth Schedule was added by the 52nd Amendment Act of 1985 to prevent elected members from defecting to other political parties. • Objective: It aims to curb political instability caused by the shifting of party loyalties by MPs and MLAs, which could lead to manipulation of the legislative process. • Under this law, any member of Parliament or State Legislature can be disqualified if they voluntarily give up their party membership or violate party directives during a vote. • The power to decide on disqualification under the Anti-Defection Law rests with the Speaker or Chairman of the respective legislative house.

questions.question: Which was the only state in India that had its own separate constitution?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Jammu and Kashmir. Before its reorganization into Union Territories in 2019, Jammu and Kashmir was the only state in India to have its own separate constitution, which was adopted in 1956. Special Status: • Jammu and Kashmir (J&K) had a unique status within the Indian Union, granted by Article 370 of the Indian Constitution, which allowed it to have its own constitution and laws. Separate Constitution: • The State Constituent Assembly of J&K adopted its own constitution on November 17, 1956, which came into effect on January 26, 1957. Abrogation of Article 370: • On August 5, 2019, the Indian government abrogated Article 370, leading to the reorganization of J&K into the Union Territories of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh, effectively ending the state's special status and its separate constitution.

questions.question: Who is the head of the Council of Ministers?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Prime Minister. The head of the Council of Ministers in India is the Prime Minister. The Prime Minister is appointed by the President of India and is typically the leader of the party that has the majority in the Lok Sabha (Lower House of Parliament). The Prime Minister leads the executive branch of the government and represents the nation in various matters of national and international significance. The Prime Minister is responsible for formulating and implementing government policies, coordinating the functioning of various ministries, and advising the President on key decisions.

questions.question: Which ocean is made up of maximum submerged islands?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Pacific Ocean The Pacific Ocean has the maximum number of submerged islands, seamounts, and atolls due to its vast size and tectonic activity in the “Ring of Fire.” The Atlantic Ocean, though the second-largest, has fewer submerged islands, mainly along the Mid-Atlantic Ridge. The Indian Ocean contains some island groups like Lakshadweep and Maldives but fewer underwater features compared to the Pacific. The Antarctic Ocean (Southern Ocean) surrounds Antarctica and is mostly deep, icy waters with limited island chains or submerged structures.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a mineral?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. None of the above. All three — Mica, Feldspar, and Quartz — are naturally occurring inorganic substances with a definite chemical composition and crystalline structure, which are the key characteristics of minerals. Mica is known for its shiny, sheet-like layers that easily peel apart, often used in electrical insulators. Feldspar is the most abundant mineral group in Earth's crust, essential in rock formation and used in ceramics and glass. Quartz is made of silicon dioxide (SiO₂) and is valued for its hardness and resistance to weathering, commonly found in sand and used in glassmaking and electronics.

questions.question: How many planets are there in our solar system?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Eight There are eight recognized planets in our solar system. In order from the Sun, they are: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. Pluto was once considered the ninth planet but was reclassified as a dwarf planet by the International Astronomical Union (IAU) in 2006 due to its size and orbital characteristics. Therefore, the total number of planets currently acknowledged in our solar system is eight.

questions.question: Which is the oldest mountain range in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Aravalli . The Aravalli Range is the oldest mountain range in India. • The Aravalli Range is located in the north-western part of India, primarily in Rajasthan. • It is considered one of the oldest mountain ranges in the world, with an estimated age of over 2.5 billion years. • The Aravallis are formed of metamorphic rocks and are much older than other major mountain ranges in India like the Himalayas.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a cause of soil erosion?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Afforestation. Afforestation (planting trees) is not a cause of soil erosion—it actually prevents erosion by: • Stabilising soil with roots. • Reducing wind/water impact. • Improving water absorption. Why Other Options Cause Erosion: • Drought – Dries soil, making it loose and prone to wind erosion. • Floods – Wash away topsoil due to excessive water flow. • Landslides – Mass movement of soil and rocks, stripping land cover. Afforestation is a solution to erosion, not a cause.

questions.question: Under the Income Tax Act, what is the due date for filing returns for a company which does not have any international transactions?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 30 November The due date for filing income tax returns under the Income Tax Act for a company without any international transactions or transfer pricing obligations is 30th September of the assessment year. Companies engaged in international transactions or subject to transfer pricing rules have an extended deadline of 30th November. Filing by the due date ensures compliance with tax laws, helps avoid penalties, and allows the company to claim deductions or carry forward losses for future assessments.

questions.question: Income tax is calculated on a/an _____ basis.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1, i.e. annual. The correct answer is Option 1, i.e. annual. • Income tax is calculated on an annual basis, i.e., the total income earned during a financial year (from April 1st to March 31st) is considered for tax calculation. • The income of an individual or entity is aggregated for the entire year, and the income tax is calculated based on the applicable tax rates for that period. • The financial year (also referred to as the Assessment Year's preceding year) is taken into account for income tax purposes. • The tax slabs defined by the Income Tax Department are applied to an individual's total income to determine the tax liability. • Taxpayers are required to file their income tax returns annually, providing details of income, expenses, and deductions for the entire year. • Therefore, income tax is calculated on an annual basis, making Option 1 the correct answer.

questions.question: When was the first five-year plan implemented in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2, i.e. 1951. • The first Five-Year Plan in India was implemented in 1951. • The primary focus of this plan was agricultural development and irrigation projects. • It aimed to address the prevailing food crisis and improve the living standards of the population. Additional Information about the First Five-Year Plan: • Period: 1951-1956 • Architect: It was drafted by a team of economists, led by P. C. Mahalanobis, though the overall direction was set by Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru. • Key Focus Areas: • Agriculture: Top priority, with significant investments in irrigation projects like the Bhakra Dam. • Power: Development of hydroelectric projects. • Transport and Communication: Expansion of roads and railways. • Model: It was largely based on the Harrod-Domar growth model, which emphasised investment in capital goods for economic growth. • Achievements: • Significant increase in agricultural production, leading to improved food availability. • Rise in national income and per capita income. • Establishment of the basic infrastructure for future industrialisation.

questions.question: Gross Domestic Product less Depreciation is ________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Net Domestic Product. Net Domestic Product (NDP) is derived by subtracting depreciation from Gross Domestic Product (GDP). GDP represents the total value of goods and services produced within a country's borders. Depreciation accounts for the wear and tear of capital assets. Therefore, NDP provides a more accurate measure of a nation's economic output, reflecting the actual value generated after considering asset depreciation.

questions.question: On which date was the Goods and Services Tax (GST) implemented in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 1 July 2017. • Purpose of GST: To integrate various indirect taxes in India, such as central excise duty, state VAT, service tax, etc. • Need for GST: The aim was to implement a single tax system, making business operations simpler and more transparent. • Key Benefits: • Eliminating the need for different taxes by the central and state governments. • Implementing a uniform tax rate for businesses across the country. • Increasing ease of doing business and enhancing transparency. • Types of GST: • CGST (Central GST): Collected by the central government. • SGST (State GST): Collected by the state governments. • IGST (Integrated GST): Applied on inter-state trade. • Outcome: The implementation of GST created a unified and structured market in India, improving tax collection and simplifying business processes.

questions.question: The double displacement reaction among the following is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. CuSO₄(aq)+H₂S(g) \(\rightarrow\)CuS(s) + H₂SO₄ (aq). The correct double displacement reaction is: CuSO₄(aq) + H₂S(g) → CuS(s) + H₂SO₄(aq). In this reaction, copper sulfate (CuSO₄) reacts with hydrogen sulfide (H₂S), resulting in the formation of copper sulfide (CuS) as a precipitate and sulfuric acid (H₂SO₄) in aqueous form. A double displacement reaction involves the exchange of ions between two compounds. The other reactions listed are examples of single displacement reactions, not double displacement.

questions.question: Which of the following metals is most metallic in nature?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Cs. Cesium is located in the alkali metal group and is the heaviest among the given metals (Li, Na, Rb, Cs). As you move down the group in the periodic table, the metallic character increases. This is due to the increased size of the atom and the weakening of the force of attraction between the nucleus and the outermost electron, making it easier to lose the electron. Therefore, Cs exhibits the most metallic character.

questions.question: Work done by a passenger standing on a platform holding a suitcase weighing 10 kg is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 0 J. Work is defined as the product of the force applied to an object and the displacement of the object in the direction of the force. The formula for work is: W=F·d·cos⁡(θ) where: • W is the work done • F is the force applied • d is the displacement • θ is the angle between the force and the direction of displacement In this case, the passenger is standing on the platform holding a suitcase, so the displacement (d) is 0. Regardless of the force applied, if there is no displacement, the work done is zero. Therefore, the work done by the passenger is: W= 10 kg×0 m= 0 J So, the work done is 0 J.

questions.question: Which of the following devices is/are based on heating effect of electric current? (I) Incandescent lamp (ii) Electric geyser (iii) Electric generator
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Both (i) and (ii) The devices based on the heating effect of electric current are: (I) Incandescent lamp: It uses the heating effect to produce light. The filament heats up to a high temperature when current passes through it, emitting light. (ii) Electric geyser: This device uses electric current to heat water. The heating element inside the geyser heats up due to the flow of current. (iii) Electric generator: This is not based on the heating effect of electric current. It converts mechanical energy into electrical energy via electromagnetic induction, not heating. Thus, (I) and (ii) are based on the heating effect.

questions.question: Where in the human body is the pylorus located?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Stomach. The pylorus is located in the digestive system, specifically at the lower end of the stomach. It connects the stomach to the small intestine, particularly the duodenum. The pylorus acts as a valve, controlling the passage of partially digested food (chyme) from the stomach into the small intestine. It prevents the backflow of intestinal contents and regulates the timing and flow of digestion. Its muscular structure helps ensure that food is properly processed before moving further in the digestive tract.

questions.question: Which of the following kingdoms consists of prokaryotic cells?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Monera. Monera is the kingdom that consists of prokaryotic cells, which lack a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. This kingdom includes bacteria and cyanobacteria (blue-green algae). Other Options: • Animalia: Composed of eukaryotic cells (with a nucleus and organelles). They are multicellular and heterotrophic. • Protista: Contains eukaryotic organisms, mostly unicellular (e.g., amoeba, algae). They are more complex than prokaryotes. • Fungi: Also eukaryotic, including molds, yeasts, and mushrooms. They have chitin in their cell walls and are heterotrophic. Monera is the only kingdom among these that is exclusively prokaryotic, making it the correct choice.

questions.question: The amount of________________ in a plant cell alters its structure in order to facilitate movement.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. water. The amount of water in a plant cell directly affects its turgor pressure, which alters cell structure to enable movement. Key examples include: • Stomatal opening/closing (regulated by water flow into guard cells) • Leaf movements (e.g., Mimosa pudica's touch response) • Growth adjustments (e.g., phototropism and gravitropism) When water enters, cells become rigid (turgid); when water exits, they shrink (flaccid). This hydraulic mechanism drives most plant movements. Other Options: • Proteins/Hormones – Facilitate signaling but don’t directly change cell shape. • Electro-chemical impulses – Found in rapid responses (e.g., Venus flytrap) but not general movement.

questions.question: In the plant kingdom, 'Ferns and Fern allies' belong to which group?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Pteridophyta. • Pteridophyte is a vascular plant with a xylem and phloem that disperses spores. • Pteridophytes produce neither flowers nor seeds, they are referred to as "cryptogams". • Pteridophyta includes ferns, horsetails and lycophytes (clubmosses, spikemosses, and quillworts). • Pteridophytes are used as cattle feed and medicine also.

questions.question: Which is not a font style ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Superscript. font style • Regular • arial • Times of roman

questions.question: which of the following is a possible error recovery technique on a simplex data link?
questions.explanation: the correct answer is option 4 i.e the use of hamming codes. • Hamming code is an error correction system that can detect and correct errors when data is stored or transmitted. • It requires adding additional parity bits with the data. It is commonly used in error correction code (ECC) RAM. • The hamming codes is an error recovery technique that can be used to detect and correct the errors and on a simplex data link, this is a possible error recovery technique. • This technique was developed by R.W. Hamming.

questions.question: What are the features of Microsoft Windows?
options: Windows control computer access to users for computer’s security.
Windows allows its user to interact with the system (using a keyboard, mouse, microphone, etc.).
It controls memory allocation, data storage in a computer system.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e all of the above. Below are some of the windows’ key benefits and features: • Windows allows its user to interact with the system (using a keyboard, mouse, microphone, etc.). • It controls memory allocation, data storage in a computer system. • It controls hardware’s incorporated into the computer system such as scanners and printers, etc. • Windows control computer access to users for computer’s security.

questions.question: A ____________ is an input device consisting of a stick that pivots on a base and reports its angle or direction to the device it is controlling.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Joystick. • A joystick is an input device consisting of a stick that pivots on a base and reports its angle or direction to the device it is controlling. • Joysticks are often used to control video games, and usually have one or more push-buttons whose state can also be read by the computer.

questions.question: Who among the following was the first singer to be presented with India’s highest civilian honour, the Bharat Ratna award?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. MS Subbulakshmi. MS Subbulakshmi was the first singer to be awarded the Bharat Ratna. She received the Bharat Ratna in 1998, a significant recognition for her contribution to Indian music and culture. • Lata Mangeshkar – Awarded Bharat Ratna in 2001 • Bhimsen Joshi – Awarded Bharat Ratna in 2009 • Bombay Jayashri – Has not received the Bharat Ratna (as of 2025)

questions.question: 'Ahirai’ is a folk dance of the Bharia tribe of ________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Madhya Pradesh. • The folk dance 'Ahirai' is traditionally performed by the Bharia tribe, an indigenous community residing primarily in the Chhindwara and Betul districts of Madhya Pradesh. • This dance form is a vibrant expression of joy, typically showcased during festivals, community gatherings, and seasonal celebrations. Other Options Explained: • Nagaland: Known for dances of tribes like Ao, Sumi, and Angami, especially during the Hornbill Festival. • Uttarakhand: Famous for folk dances like Barada Nati and Langvir Nritya. • Karnataka: Renowned for Yakshagana and Dollu Kunitha, not Ahirai.

questions.question: Which of the following deities is worshipped on the occasion of the Malayalam New Year?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Lord Vishnu. On the occasion of the Malayalam New Year, known as Vishu, Lord Vishnu is worshipped, particularly in the form of Lord Krishna. Vishu, celebrated in Kerala during April, marks the beginning of the astronomical New Year. The festival features the Vishukkani, an auspicious arrangement of items like rice, fruits, gold, and an idol of Lord Krishna, which is the first sight seen in the morning. Devotees visit temples, offer prayers, and seek blessings for prosperity and good fortune.

questions.question: ‘Karagam’ is a folk dance of which of the following states of India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Tamil Nadu. • Karagam is a traditional folk dance of Tamil Nadu, performed in honour of the rain goddess Mariamman and River Goddess Gangai Amman. • Dancers balance ornate pots (karagams) on their heads while performing intricate steps, often accompanied by live music and devotional songs. • It is especially popular during temple festivals and rural celebrations. Other Options Explained: • Odisha: Known for classical dance Odissi and folk forms like Dalkhai and Ghumura. • Rajasthan: Famous for Ghoomar and Kalbelia dances. • Assam: Renowned for Bihu and Bagurumba dances.

questions.question: Where is the headquarters of the National Remote Sensing Centre (NRSC) situated?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Hyderabad. The headquarters of the National Remote Sensing Centre (NRSC) is situated in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. NRSC is a key center of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), responsible for remote sensing satellite data acquisition, processing, and applications. It plays a crucial role in earth observation, disaster management, and natural resource monitoring. NRSC operates multiple ground stations and collaborates with various national and international organizations to provide satellite imagery and geospatial solutions.

questions.question: Annapurna Devi, the legendary musician awarded with Padma Bhushan in 1977, was associated with which of the following musical instruments?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is opiton 1 i.e. Surbahar • Annapurna Devi, the legendary musician awarded with Padma Bhushan in 1977, was associated with the musical instruments of Surbahar. • She received the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award in the year 1991 and was made a fellow of the Sangeet Natak Akademi in 2004.

questions.question: Which of the following monasteries is NOT located in Sikkim?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Tabo. The Tabo Monastery, a significant Buddhist complex, is located in the Spiti Valley of Himachal Pradesh, India, and not in Sikkim. It's one of the oldest continuously operating Buddhist monasteries in India and holds immense historical and cultural significance. Sikkim, while rich in monasteries, does not house the Tabo Monastery. This distinction is important for accurately locating and understanding the heritage of these religious sites.

questions.question: Bara Imambara, a historical edifice with marvellous architecture, is situated in which of the following cities?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Lucknow • Bara Imambara, a historical edifice with marvellous architecture, is situated in the city of Lucknow. • This Imambara was built by Nawab Asaf-ud-Daula in the year 1784 and its designer was Kifayat-Ullah, who is said to be a relative of the architect of the Taj Mahal. • This Imambara is also referred to as Asafi Imambara.

questions.question: When is International Labour Day observed every year?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. May 1. International Labour Day 2025, observed on May 1, celebrates workers' contributions globally while highlighting workers' rights, fair practices, and safe workplaces. Rooted in the 19th-century labour movement, it commemorates struggles for fair work conditions, such as the 1886 U.S. strike. In India, it has been celebrated since 1923. The day also emphasises modern challenges like automation and informal employment. In India, key labour laws have been consolidated into four codes, reinforcing workers' rights to fair wages, social security, and safe conditions.

questions.question: Where was the Purple Fest 2025 held recently to promote inclusivity and empowerment for persons with disabilities?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. New Delhi. The Purple Fest 2025, held at Rashtrapati Bhavan, New Delhi aimed to promote inclusivity and empowerment for persons with disabilities. With 23,500 attendees, including President Droupadi Murmu and key officials, the event featured cultural performances, literary discussions, and adaptive sports like Blind Cricket and Wheelchair Basketball. Corporate collaborations with organizations like Tata Power and Tech Mahindra Foundation focused on education, skilling, and employment. MoUs were signed to enhance accessibility and foster inclusive growth, emphasizing economic empowerment for Divyangjan.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the Joint Secretary of the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Anuj Kumar Singh. Anuj Kumar Singh, an officer belonging to the Indian Railway Service of Electrical Engineers (IRSEE) from the 2009 batch, has been appointed as the Joint Secretary (Director level) in the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC). This appointment was made under the Central Staffing Scheme and is for a tenure of five years or until further orders. Singh's expertise and experience from the railway sector are expected to contribute to the efficient functioning and strategic framework of the UPSC. His selection for this central deputation was recommended by the Ministry of Railways. He has been directed to assume his new responsibilities at the UPSC with immediate effect.

questions.question: Where was WAVES 2025, the World Audio Visual & Entertainment Summit, launched recently?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Mumbai. WAVES 2025, the World Audio Visual & Entertainment Summit, launched on May 1, 2025, in Mumbai, aims to position India as a global leader in digital content, animation, and cinematic arts. Organised by the Indian government, it attracted over 100,000 registrations from 60+ countries. The summit highlights innovation, featuring contests, global speakers, esports tournaments, cultural events, and startup exhibitions. It fosters the creator economy, enhances India's soft power, and integrates cutting-edge technologies like AI, XR, and VFX into creative storytelling.

questions.question: Which country has developed the first genome-edited rice varieties?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. India. The country that has developed the first genome-edited rice varieties is India. Recently, on May 4, 2025, India's Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan launched the first two genome-edited rice varieties developed by the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) institutions: 'DRR Dhan 100 (Kamala)' and 'Pusa DST Rice 1'. These varieties leverage CRISPR-Cas technology to enhance traits like yield, drought tolerance, and salinity tolerance without introducing foreign DNA, marking a significant milestone in agricultural biotechnology. Other options: • China: Chinese researchers have utilized genome editing tools like CRISPR-Cas to develop disease-resistant rice varieties. However, these are primarily in the research and trial phases and have not been released for commercial cultivation. • USA: The United States has made significant advancements in genome editing technologies, particularly with CRISPR-Cas9. While various crops have been developed using these technologies, there is no record of the USA releasing genome-edited rice varieties for commercial cultivation. • Japan: Japan has also engaged in genome editing research and has some commercialized genome-edited produce (like a high-GABA tomato). However, India holds the distinction of having developed and released the first genome-edited rice varieties.

questions.question: Who was re-elected as the Prime Minister of Australia in 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Anthony Albanese. Anthony Albanese secured a historic re-election as Australia’s Prime Minister in 2025, leading the Labour Party to a landslide victory despite economic challenges and global anti-incumbency trends. This marks the first back-to-back win for an Australian PM in over 20 years. The result reflected public support for fairness, opportunity, and stable governance. Opposition leader Peter Dutton lost his seat amid criticism of “Trump-like” tactics. Labour’s second term aims to address housing, healthcare, climate change, and Indigenous reconciliation.

questions.question: Which Indian city has implemented initiatives like cool roofs and tree plantations to combat rising temperatures?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Ahmedabad. • Ahmedabad has taken the lead in combating rising temperatures by implementing cool roof initiatives and extensive tree plantation drives. • Under its Heat Action Plan, cool roofs coated with reflective white paint are used in slum areas and public buildings, reducing indoor temperatures by 2–5°C. • These measures began after the deadly 2010 heatwave, making Ahmedabad a model for urban climate resilience. Similar Initiatives in Other Cities Jaipur – Green Rajasthan Campaign • Focus: Urban afforestation and tree plantations to combat desertification and heat. • The Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) initiated mass tree plantations in urban areas and parks under this campaign. Chennai – Singara Chennai 2.0 (Smart City Project) • Focuses on urban greening, restoring water bodies, and creating green corridors. Lucknow – Heat Resilience Action Plan (Draft Stage) • Initiated under UP Smart City Mission, Lucknow has proposed cool roof pilot projects, green spaces, and public awareness drives to tackle heatwaves.

questions.question: Which initiative has been granted income tax exemption by the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) recently?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG). • On April 28, 2025, the Investor Education and Protection Fund Authority (IEPFA) held a virtual meeting to finalise operational details for the 'Niveshak Shivir' initiative, aimed at addressing unclaimed dividends. • The pilot phase will launch in Ahmedabad and Mumbai in May 2025. • Separately, the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) granted income tax exemption status to the National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) to support the Namami Gange Programme.

questions.question: How many gold medals did India win at the 2nd Asian Yogasana Sport Championship?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 83. India won an impressive 83 gold medals at the 2nd Asian Yogasana Sport Championship held in New Delhi. This remarkable achievement placed India at the top of the medal tally, significantly ahead of other participating nations. Japan secured the second position with 3 gold medals, Mongolia and Oman each won 1 gold medal, and Nepal did not win any gold medals. India's dominant performance with 83 gold medals highlights the country's strong tradition and growing prowess in competitive Yogasana.

questions.question: Which department will release a commemorative stamp to honour the centenary of tabla legend Pandit Chatur Lal?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Department of Posts. The Department of Posts, Government of India, is set to release a commemorative stamp to honor the centenary of tabla maestro Pandit Chatur Lal. This initiative recognizes his pioneering role in introducing the tabla to Western audiences during the 1950s and 1960s through collaborations with legends like Pandit Ravi Shankar and Yehudi Menuhin . The stamp will be unveiled during the grand finale of his centenary celebrations, concluding in April 2026. Other Options: • Department of Art and Culture: While it promotes cultural heritage, it does not issue postage stamps. • Department of Finance: This department manages economic policies and finances, unrelated to postal commemorations.
